name: instant-worker — propagation_runner unexpected_skip (CHAOS F1 silent data-loss class)
description: P0 page. CHAOS F1 finding (2026-05-20): propagation_runner used to silently mark APPLIED on any row whose target resource was missing/in an unexpected state. The fix landed the instant_propagation_unexpected_skip_total{kind,resource_type,skip_reason} counter and the runner now treats unexpected_skip as a Failure (counts against maxAttempts) instead of a silent success. This alert is the post-fix sentinel: if a row hits a skip path more than once, the entitlement reconciler will converge eventually but the operator should investigate the underlying schema/state drift NOW because every unexpected_skip is one more class of customer-facing nondeterminism. Source: worker/internal/metrics/metrics.go (PropagationUnexpectedSkipTotal), emit site worker/internal/jobs/propagation_runner.go.

nrql.query: SELECT sum(instant_propagation_unexpected_skip_total) FROM Metric WHERE metricName = 'instant_propagation_unexpected_skip_total' FACET kind, resource_type, skip_reason
